prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `d6e50e54`.
codex_response:
`d6e50e54` is now implemented as an ARC2 task package. The rule is: recolor the single solid `1` block to `2`, then project each `9` straight toward the block along its current row or column; if its distance to that face is less than the block size on that axis it lands on the edge, otherwise it lands one cell outside. The summary hint was only directionally useful and I corrected it in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_d6e50e54/notes.md#L1), because “nearest side” is misleading here.

The generator matches the official family by sampling square `1` blocks on a `7` background with sparse, axis-aligned `9` markers at varied distances, including both edge-landing and outside-landing cases. No new `dsl_ext` primitive was needed. Validation passed end-to-end: official examples `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and the preview sheet looked on-family.
